citation: Wikipedia, "Iranian rial" article, table "Official vs. free exchange rates (rials per US dollar)", https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iranian_rial -- sourced by Wikipedia to Central Bank of Iran, "Annual Review 2013/14" (Feb 2015) and CIA World Factbook.
notes: Annual figure (CBI's own compiled annual value; exact within-year averaging/snapshot methodology not stated by Wikipedia's transcription). Official and free/parallel columns are IDENTICAL for 2003-2010 in this table -- reflects the real, well-documented fact that Iran's official and market rates were unified/converged from the March 2002 unification until sanctions-driven divergence resumed around 2011-2012 (independently corroborated by PBS Frontline/Tehran Bureau's narrative below and by this dataset's own Bahmani-Oskooee monthly series showing official near-equivalence through 2003). NOT a case of substituting the official rate for convenience -- during this specific window there was genuinely no premium to report. Primary CBI document itself (cbi.ir) is geo-blocked from this project's environment, and its Wayback Machine mirror was also inaccessible in this session's browser tool (domain-level access restriction) -- recorded via Wikipedia's transcription as the best available accessible route to a real CBI-published figure; flagged as one hop removed from the primary document, not independently re-verified against the raw PDF.

citation: Bahmani-Oskooee, Mohsen (2005), "History of the Rial and Foreign Exchange Policy in Iran," Iranian Economic Review, Vol. 10, No. 14 (Fall 2005), Table 4, p.16-19. https://ier.ut.ac.ir/article_30891.html (PDF: https://ier.ut.ac.ir/article_30891_cc9816769f0293f06d92344e31426eea.pdf) (author's personal first-hand observation, main text p.16, footnote 1)
notes: Author Bahmani-Oskooee's own personal observation of the dollar BUYING rate at an authorized bank (Bank Melli) in Iran on Aug 15, 2004. Same-day SELLING rate was 8,724 and the rate outside authorized banks (i.e. the informal/negotiated market) was 8,775 rials/dollar -- all three within 0.7% of each other, consistent with the paper's point that by 2004 the free-market and negotiated/authorized-bank rates had converged. This single dated spot observation extends real, directly-cited coverage 8 months past the source table's own Dec-2003 cutoff.
